question: A business rule task is modelled with the XML shown. Which binding type do the docs say is applied to `shipping_box_size`?

options.text: `latest`, because no `bindingType` attribute is specified on `<zeebe:calledDecision>`.
`deployment`, because the decision was deployed together with the process.
`versionTag`, with `latest` interpreted as the tag value.
No binding type is applied; the deployment is rejected because `bindingType` is required.

a.text: The chunk labels this XML as 'no explicit binding type (`latest` is used implicitly)', so the engine treats the called decision as bound by `latest`.

b.text: `deployment` requires `bindingType="deployment"` on `<zeebe:calledDecision>`. The shown XML omits the attribute, so `latest` is used implicitly.

c.text: `versionTag` requires both `bindingType="versionTag"` and a separate `versionTag` attribute; the literal string `latest` is not interpreted as a tag value.

d.text: `bindingType` is optional. When omitted, the docs note that `latest` is used implicitly rather than rejecting the deployment.

explanation: The XML representation in the docs labels this first example as 'no explicit binding type (`latest` is used implicitly)'. The engine therefore applies the `latest` binding to the called decision when the task is activated.

codeBlocks.stem:
<bpmn:businessRuleTask id="determine-box-size" name="Determine shipping box size">
  <bpmn:extensionElements>
    <zeebe:calledDecision decisionId="shipping_box_size" resultVariable="boxSize" />
  </bpmn:extensionElements>
</bpmn:businessRuleTask>
